text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring code quality. Unit tests check individual components in isolation, helping developers catch bugs early. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work together correctly. Many developers practice test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach encourages clear thinking and better design. Ultimately, comprehensive testing builds confidence in the software, allowing teams to make changes and updates without fear of breaking existing functionality. When you know your tests cover the main scenarios, you can release new features with greater peace of mind.
